title_guess: Devizes & Foxhanger (Kennet & Avon Canal) with Charles, Jenny & John

pages.text:
& get into a field of sheep. Ghastly. Simon just caught him before he chased a horse. Dreadful. We had lunch & left. A good camp site but poor blackberrying.

__Friday September 28th__
	Simon, Eve & Tinder left at 5 to go to __Devizes__ to spend the w/end with Charles & Jenny (arrived 2.15. Sat pm) & John (arrived 3.15. Sat.).
	We arrived about 8 & had bacon sandwiches. We're at __Foxhangar__ where in the 70's, as the canal was being repaired, a farm diversified into camp site & large marina. Lovely big open spaces. Distant sound of road. Clean loos.
__Saturday__ With Tinder on a harness we cycled up an enormous flight of locks 1796-1810. to Devizes & looked around the town. Nice, Georgian, shabby Cheltenham. We had coffee & cycled back to the van & [juked]

[Glued-in leaflet: "Explore the Magic of Our Kennet & Avon Canal" — Kennet & Avon Canal Trust, with aerial photograph of a flight of canal locks. Anniversary badge '50' visible.]

our campsite

nearly half a bucket of blackberries. After John arrived we cycled back up the locks & back again. We had booked supper at the Bear for 7.30. In the town, earlier, we had chatted to a man selling wind turbines from Iddea.
	I cycled the 11k to [?Denies] & back but Simon didn't need to cycle. He just got towed by Tinder who seems to be part huskie.
	We had a good evening meal. John had 16oz rare T bone steak. Charles had 8oz steak. Pollock, duck, sardinos
	__Sunday__
	The night wasn't as cold as the [strikethrough: per] previous one. We gave Jenny a hot water bottle. After breakfast we cycled 6½ miles towards Bradford, but there was a head wind so we didn't get there - We turned back. Tinder refused to ride in the knapsack or bike bag & we didn't think he should be running so
